content.problemStatement:
You are one of the notorious coal mafias. You run an illegal coal mining business. There are nnn villages, enumerated from 111 to nnn from left to right. Each village has some coal mines. This information is given to you by an integer coalicoal_icoali​ denoting the amount of coal (in kgs) in village iii. The cost of coal is fixed at 1 rupee per kg.

There are toll booths in the highway at the middle of the road connecting any two adjacent villages. So, if you want to go from village iii to village i+1i + 1i+1, you might have to pay toll tax for it. This information is provided you by an integer taxitax_itaxi​ which denotes the toll tax to pay on the road joining villages iii and i+1i + 1i+1. Additionally, some of the toll booths allow you to go both to and fro and pay tax only once, while others don't. This is provided you by an integer policyipolicy_ipolicyi​ which will be either 1 or 2, denoting whether the toll booth allows 1 way taxation or 2 way taxation. If a toll booth has 1 way taxation, you can cross it twice by paying tax for only the first time you cross the road, you don't need to pay tax the second time. On other hand, if it has two way taxation, then you will have to pay toll tax both the times you cross the road.

You brought a big truck and now want to collect coal in turn maximizing your profit. You will start your journey at village 1, and collect coal from some of the villages and then end back at village 1 itself. Compute the maximum profit you can obtain!


Input Format
First line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.


The first line of each test case a single integer nnn denoting the number of villages.


The second line of each test case contains nnn space separated integers in which the iii-th of them denotes coalicoal_icoali​ - amount of coal in village iii.


The third line of each test case contains n−1n - 1n−1 space separated integers in which the iii-th of them denotes taxitax_itaxi​ - amount of toll tax you have to pay each time you cross the road between village iii and i+1i + 1i+1.


The fourth line of each test case contains n−1n - 1n−1 space separated integers in which the iii-th of them denotes policyipolicy_ipolicyi​ - whether the toll booth charges 1 way taxation or 2 way while using the road between village iii and i+1i + 1i+1.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer in a separate line corresponding to the maximum profit that you can obtain.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n denoting the number of villages. The second line contains n space-separated integers denoting the amount of coal in each village. The third line contains n-1 space-separated integers denoting the toll tax for each road. The fourth line contains n-1 space-separated integers denoting the policy for each road.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer denoting the maximum profit that can be obtained.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: For the first test case, the maximum profit is 1 which can be obtained by collecting coal from the first village only. For the second test case, the maximum profit is 2 which can be obtained by collecting coal from the second village only.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We can create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] denotes the maximum profit that can be obtained by visiting the villages from i to j. We can fill up this array by iterating over all possible pairs of villages and considering the toll tax and policy for each road.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) - where T is the number of test cases and n is the number of villages.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n^2) - where n is the number of villages.

code.Python3:
def max_profit(coal, tax, policy):
    n = len(coal)
    dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        dp[i][i] = coal[i]
    for j in range(1, n):
        for i in range(n - j):
            k = i + j
            dp[i][k] = max(dp[i][k], dp[i][k - 1] + coal[k] - (policy[k - 1] == 1 and tax[k - 1] or 0))
            dp[i][k] = max(dp[i][k], dp[i + 1][k] + coal[i] - (policy[i] == 1 and tax[i] or 0))
    return dp[0][n - 1]

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n = int(input())
    coal = list(map(int, input().split()))
    tax = list(map(int, input().split()))
    policy = list(map(int, input().split()))
    print(max_profit(coal, tax, policy))
